context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=3; meal=bite-sized sandwiches; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips that are not cold-served fruit/dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE the earlier observed personal meal used light reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches or other warm savory foods THEN 2 min BECAUSE the new working meal selected stronger reheating for that food type. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to protect visibility of the device on the right. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the new working meal with the laptop on the left did not prioritize view protection. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed personal meal did not prioritize occlusion. Current evidence suggests occlusion concern is selective rather than global.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a slower pace, suggesting comfort is prioritized when not occupied. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN faster-than-personal speeds are preferred BECAUSE both working meals favored more efficiency than the personal meal. IF working context AND neutral affect AND strong efficiency pressure is present THEN fast BECAUSE the earlier working meal favored the fastest option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN medium BECAUSE the new working meal selected a moderate speed, indicating efficiency can be balanced with control depending on the meal.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE earlier observed meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the new working meal selected the narrower-bite option, suggesting food geometry can outweigh the prior general preference for acquisition reliability.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE both working meals also skipped confirmation while occupied. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal signaling, suggesting a preference for salient readiness cues. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right THEN speech BECAUSE the earlier working meal used an auditory cue only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new working meal used multimodal cueing. Current evidence suggests cue preference in working contexts may depend on setup details rather than work alone.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in a personal setting. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the earlier working meal favored reduced interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE the new working meal returned to intentional user-driven initiation. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. Current evidence indicates the user balances efficiency and explicit timing control within working contexts.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal meal used this low-effort but still intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the earlier working meal favored faster automated flow. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE the new working meal selected intentional user-driven sip initiation. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. Current evidence indicates the user may switch between automation and explicit low-effort control depending on the working situation.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal meal used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both working meals also used automation. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal completion cues. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right THEN speech BECAUSE the earlier working meal used auditory completion cueing only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new working meal used multimodal completion cueing. Current evidence suggests completion-cue preference in working contexts may depend on setup details rather than work alone.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head control and consistently selected perception-based completion for drinking; button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head control and consistently selected perception-based completion for mouth wiping; button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal meal kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE both working meals also prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF foods include sauces or dips AND working context AND neutral affect THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed working meal with ketchup avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed fruit meal also used no dipping. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods without clear dipping use, such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the new working meal also selected no dipping. No context-dependent variation beyond food availability and the observed working cases is supported yet.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a longer wait, suggesting the user wants more time to respond when not rushed by another task. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE both working meals used the shortest wait, consistent with strong efficiency preferences while occupied. This indicates a stable context-dependent tradeoff between responsiveness and efficiency.
